description_long: This is a stylized, single-propeller airplane with a compact fuselage and a distinctive green and tan color scheme. The wings are straight and low-mounted, featuring black cross insignias reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The engine is a prominent, exposed inline piston type mounted at the front, with visible exhaust pipes and a wooden propeller. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, with a small rear wheel or skid for landing support. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the engine, likely designed for a single pilot, and the landing gear consists of two main fixed wheels under the wings and a tail skid. The overall design suggests a vintage, possibly World War I-inspired aircraft, suitable for display or thematic urban environments.
description: A vintage-style, single-propeller airplane with green and tan coloring, exposed engine, and open cockpit. Features low-mounted straight wings with black cross insignias and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the exposed engine, wooden propeller, open cockpit, and detailed tail section with cross insignia.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wooden propeller, engine detail, and the full span of the straight, low-mounted wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the engine, cockpit, and tail, with clear visibility of the cross insignia and landing gear.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizers, and the alignment of the wings and landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible dihedral or anhedral angle, no winglets, and squared wingtips.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted exposed inline piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a rear skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the engine, with no visible canopy.
